questions.question: What do we call the process by which plants make their own food?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis! Plants use sunlight, water and air to make their own food. That is why they need sunlight.

questions.question: Which of these is a source of light?
questions.explanation: The Sun produces its own light — it is a light source. Mirrors and windows reflect or let light through but do not make it.

questions.question: What are the three states of matter?
questions.explanation: The three states of matter are Solid, Liquid and Gas. Water can be all three: ice (solid), water (liquid), steam (gas)!

questions.question: What organ pumps blood around your body?
questions.explanation: Your heart pumps blood all around your body. It beats about 70 times every minute!

questions.question: Which force pulls objects towards the ground?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls everything towards the Earth. That is why things fall down when you drop them!

questions.question: What do herbivores eat?
questions.explanation: Herbivores, such as rabbits and cows, eat only plants. Carnivores eat meat, and omnivores eat both.

questions.question: Which planet do we live on?
questions.explanation: We live on Earth, the third planet from the Sun. It is the only planet known to have life.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of water?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level. We use this to make tea and cook pasta!

questions.question: Which of these is a solid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Wood is a solid — it holds its shape. Water and milk are liquids, and air is a gas.

questions.question: What do we call an animal that eats both plants and meat?
questions.explanation: An omnivore eats both plants and animals. Humans are omnivores, and so are bears and foxes.

questions.question: What gas do humans breathe out?
questions.explanation: We breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide. Plants use the CO2 we breathe out!

questions.question: Which of these is a mammal?
questions.explanation: A dolphin is a mammal — it breathes air, is warm-blooded, and feeds its young with milk. Fish, birds and frogs are not mammals.

questions.question: What is the closest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to Earth! It is about 150 million kilometres away. Other stars are much, much farther.

questions.question: Which part of the plant soaks up water from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant in soil and absorb water and nutrients. The stem then carries water up to the leaves.

questions.question: How many legs does an insect have?
questions.explanation: All insects have exactly 6 legs. Ants, butterflies and beetles are all insects. Spiders have 8 legs and are arachnids, not insects.

questions.question: What type of rock is formed from layers of mud and sand pressed together?
questions.explanation: Sedimentary rock forms when layers of sediment (mud, sand, shells) are pressed together over millions of years. Sandstone is an example.

questions.question: What does a thermometer measure?
questions.explanation: A thermometer measures temperature in degrees Celsius. We use them to check the weather and take our temperature when ill.

questions.question: Which of these can a magnet attract?
questions.explanation: Magnets attract materials that contain iron, such as steel paperclips. Plastic, wood and glass are not magnetic.

questions.question: What is the name for the bones that protect your brain?
questions.explanation: The skull is the set of bones that surrounds and protects your brain. It is also called the cranium.

questions.question: What causes shadows to form?
answers: Light being blocked by an object
questions.explanation: Shadows form when an opaque object blocks light. The shadow appears on the opposite side to the light source.
